description: Fix side panel UX gaps in state communication and error handling: add reconnecting state feedback, loading text, search clear button aria-label, empty connected state message, longer error auto-dismiss duration, and npm search error display.

userStories.title: Add loading text to initial loading state
userStories.description: The LoadingState component shows only bouncing dots with no text. Add 'Connecting...' text below the loader so the user knows the side panel is initializing, not broken.
acceptanceCriteria: LoadingState renders text 'Connecting...' below the Loader
Text uses font-sans text-sm text-muted-foreground, centered
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/EmptyStates.tsx (modify LoadingState) Context: The current LoadingState at line 70 is minimal: ```tsx const LoadingState = () => ; ``` It is rendered in App.tsx line 401 when `loading` is true, centered via the parent `
` having `flex min-h-full items-center justify-center`. Changes: Wrap the Loader in a flex column container with centered text below: ```tsx const LoadingState = () => (
Connecting...
); ``` Constraints: - Do NOT change the Loader component itself. - Do NOT change App.tsx — the parent already centers the content. - Match the typography conventions in the file: `text-muted-foreground` for secondary text, `text-sm` for body.

userStories.title: Add aria-label to search clear button
userStories.description: The X button that clears the search field has no accessible name. Screen readers announce it as an unlabeled button with an SVG.
acceptanceCriteria: The search clear button has aria-label="Clear search"
No other changes to the search bar behavior
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (add aria-label to clear button)

Context:
The search clear button at App.tsx lines 378-384:
```tsx
<button
  type="button"
  onClick={() => handleSearchChange('')}
  className="absolute top-1/2 right-2 -translate-y-1/2 cursor-pointer text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground">
  <X className="h-4 w-4" />
</button>
```

Changes:
Add `aria-label="Clear search"` to the button element.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the button's position, styling, or click behavior.
- Do NOT add any other ARIA attributes.

userStories.title: Add reconnecting feedback to disconnected state
userStories.description: When the server is unreachable, the side panel shows a static 'Cannot Reach MCP Server' screen with no indication that the extension is auto-reconnecting in the background. Add a subtle reconnecting indicator so the user knows the system is working.
acceptanceCriteria: ConnectionRefusedState shows a reconnecting indicator below the command text
The indicator uses the Loader component with text 'Reconnecting...'
AuthFailedState is NOT modified (auth failure does not auto-reconnect)
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/EmptyStates.tsx (modify ConnectionRefusedState) Context: The ConnectionRefusedState at lines 7-47 renders an Empty card with title, separator, description, and command code block. The offscreen document auto-reconnects with exponential backoff, but the UI gives no indication of this. Current structure: ```tsx Cannot Reach MCP Server Start the MCP server: {command} ``` Changes: Add a reconnecting indicator below the code block, still inside ``: ```tsx
Reconnecting...
``` The Loader component (retro/Loader.tsx) supports a `variant="muted"` prop that renders dots in `bg-muted-foreground` color — this is appropriate for a subtle secondary indicator. Import `Loader` from `./retro/Loader.js`. Constraints: - Do NOT modify AuthFailedState — auth failures require manual action (extension reload), not auto-reconnect. - Do NOT modify the Empty primitive. - Do NOT add any reconnection logic — this is purely a visual indicator that the existing background reconnection is happening. - Keep the destructive color scheme for the main card — the reconnecting indicator is muted to distinguish it from the error state.

userStories.title: Show empty state when connected with no plugins
userStories.description: When connected but no plugins are installed and no search query is active, the user sees a blank area above the footer. Add a friendly empty state message prompting the user to search for plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: When connected, no plugins, no failed plugins, no search query, and browser tools are the only content, a message appears below the browser tools
The message uses the Empty primitive with text like 'No plugins installed' and a prompt to search
The message does NOT appear when a search query is active
The message does NOT appear when any plugins (including failed) exist
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (add empty state in render) Context: In App.tsx lines 424-449, the `hasContent` condition at line 341: ```tsx const hasContent = plugins.length > 0 || failedPlugins.length > 0 || browserTools.length > 0; ``` Since `browserTools` is always populated from `BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG`, `hasContent` is always true when connected. The issue is that when there are no plugins, the user sees only the Browser card with nothing below it — no indication that plugins can be added. The render logic at lines 424-449: ```tsx ) : hasContent ? ( <> {browserTools.length > 0 && ( )} ) : null} ``` Changes: Add a condition after PluginList: when `plugins.length === 0 && failedPlugins.length === 0`, render a lightweight hint. Do NOT use the full `Empty` card (that would look like an error). Instead, use a subtle centered text prompt: ```tsx {plugins.length === 0 && failedPlugins.length === 0 && (

No plugins installed

Search to discover and install plugins

)} ``` This matches the existing "No results" text pattern at SearchResults.tsx line 147: ```tsx {showNoResults &&
No results
} ``` Constraints: - Do NOT change the `hasContent` variable — browser tools ensure it is always true when connected. - Do NOT show this when plugins exist (even if all are failed). - Do NOT show this during search mode — SearchResults handles its own empty state. - Keep it visually subtle — this is a hint, not an error.

userStories.title: Increase error auto-dismiss duration
userStories.description: The ERROR_DISPLAY_DURATION_MS constant is 3000ms for all error types. This is too aggressive — users often miss the error flash. Increase to 5000ms.
acceptanceCriteria: All error auto-dismiss timers in App.tsx, PluginCard.tsx, and BrowserToolsCard.tsx automatically use the new value (they all import this constant)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/constants.ts (change value)

Context:
The constant at constants.ts line 2:
```tsx
export const ERROR_DISPLAY_DURATION_MS = 3000;
```

It is imported by:
- App.tsx line 33: `import { ERROR_DISPLAY_DURATION_MS } from './constants.js';` (used at line 111)
- PluginCard.tsx line 10: `import { ERROR_DISPLAY_DURATION_MS } from '../constants.js';` (used at line 60)
- BrowserToolsCard.tsx line 7: `import { ERROR_DISPLAY_DURATION_MS } from '../constants.js';` (used at line 74)

Changes:
Change the value from 3000 to 5000:
```tsx
export const ERROR_DISPLAY_DURATION_MS = 5000;
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any file other than constants.ts — all consumers import the constant.
- Do NOT change TOOL_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT_MS.
- Do NOT add different durations for different error severities — that complexity is not justified yet.

userStories.title: Show error message when npm search fails
userStories.description: When the npm registry search fails (network error, rate limit), results silently disappear. Show a 'Search failed' message in the search results area.
acceptanceCriteria: When searchPlugins() rejects, an error state is shown in place of results
The error message is 'Search failed' displayed as muted text, similar to 'No results'
The error clears when the user types a new search query
Successful searches clear any previous error
